audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as continuation of Job's reply: spirit broken, burial imagery, request for pledge/guarantor, friends' blocked heart, righteous endurance, and hope descending to Sheol. Sensitive decay imagery is restrained while preserving the lament.

verses.restored: My spirit is broken; my days are extinguished; the burial place is before me.
Surely mockers are with me, and my eye dwells on their provocation.
Please set a pledge for me with yourself; who else will strike hands with me?
For you have hidden their heart from understanding; therefore you will not exalt them.
One who denounces friends for a share, even the eyes of his children will fail.
He has made me a proverb of peoples, and I have become one before whom they spit.
My eye has grown dim from grief, and all my limbs are like a shadow.
The upright are appalled at this, and the innocent stirs himself against the godless.
Yet the righteous holds to his way, and the one clean of hands grows stronger and stronger.
But all of you, return and come now; I will not find a wise one among you.
My days have passed; my plans are broken off, the possessions of my heart.
They make night into day; light is near, they say, in the face of darkness.
If I hope for Sheol as my house, if I spread my bed in darkness,
if I call the pit my father, and the hidden life of the grave my mother and my sister,
where then is my hope? My hope, who can see it?
Will it go down to the bars of Sheol? Shall we descend together into the dust?
